feedback.text: I've been playing games since I was 13, back in the day used to be browser Flash games and eventually moved on to bigger game platforms. Through these years I've seen cases of games dissapearing because they wouldn't be supported anymore, due to lack of resources or just being a discontinued project, leaving people with nothing of what they paid for. While this hasn't affected me in a big scale, I remember an old flash game which was strictly online. I met a few good friends there who I lost contact with because the game just shut down from one day to another and, while there was no money from my part in this, there was an actual "premium account" feature which costed real money, and which people actually bought, and which got lost when the game dissapeared. I remember checking online, searching for some way of being able to play the game back in a standalone client or something like this, but never got any luck. So obviously, when I learned about the Stop Destroying Videogames, I instantly thought on all of this and supported the initiative headfirst. Not because I wanted to recover what was lost, but to let future people who loves videogames enjoy what they paid for for as much and as long as they want.
